purpose: Map the current JSON-based sample workflow to a Flowise/Dify/Langflow-style visual builder. This is a presentation and design artifact, not a complete Flowise export.
reason_for_flowise_first: Flowise is the closest match for a quick visual workflow PoC because its AgentFlow concept focuses on explicit workflow orchestration and visual nodes.

workflow_nodes.notes: Extracts structured request context from natural language.
Stops the workflow and asks a follow-up question when city/date/purpose/style is missing.
Calls the weather tool and normalizes external data.
Loads purchase history and summarizes user preference.
Combines weather and preference data, then ranks owned and additional items.
Renders the final user-facing answer from a configured template.

acceptance_checks: The current desktop demo can run the full 6-node workflow.
The static builder preview shows the same node sequence visually.
The presentation explains that this is a sample workflow, not a completed drag-and-drop builder.
The final answer to evaluators explains why domain-specific templates are still needed even when generic builders exist.
